symptoms.symptom_name: Abrupt-onset rapid fluttering palpitations, constant for ~4 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: New-onset atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden sustained rapid palpitations are the cardinal symptom of new-onset AF with RVR.
Abrupt-onset rapid palpitations are also typical of paroxysmal SVT — discriminated only on ECG.

symptoms.symptom_name: Exertional dyspnoea (breathless walking to the bathroom)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: New-onset atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loss of atrial kick and a rapid rate reduce cardiac output, producing exertional breathlessness.
New dyspnoea with tachyarrhythmia keeps PE on the differential until excluded.
Dyspnoea can be an anginal equivalent, particularly in older adults.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: New-onset atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced cardiac output from a rapid irregular rate causes cerebral hypoperfusion and presyncope; a red flag for haemodynamic compromise.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: New-onset atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A patient-reported irregular fast pulse is suggestive but not diagnostic; the clinician-confirmed 'irregularly irregular' pulse is the stronger sign and requires palpation.
Lowers but does not exclude ACS — silent/atypical ischaemia is common in older hypertensive adults.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: New-onset atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fatigue accompanies reduced cardiac output but is non-specific.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: New-onset atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypertension is the commonest modifiable substrate for AF and contributes a point to CHA2DS2-VASc.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: New-onset atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Unmitigated cardioembolic stroke risk — the key management gap to address after CHA2DS2-VASc assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Irregularly irregular peripheral pulse with apical-radial deficit

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: New-onset atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An irregularly irregular pulse with an apical-radial deficit is the classic bedside sign of AF — requires clinician palpation, sealed from telehealth.
Flutter with fixed block gives a regular pulse; irregular irregularity argues against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Variable intensity of the first heart sound (S1)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: New-onset atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Beat-to-beat variation in diastolic filling produces variable S1 — a clinician auscultation finding, sealed from telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG: absent P waves with irregularly irregular narrow-complex rhythm

investigation_details.expected_result: Absent P waves, irregularly irregular R-R intervals, narrow QRS at ~118/min — confirmatory of atrial fibrillation (presumed, pending formal tracing)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: AF cannot be confirmed and flutter/SVT/ischaemia cannot be distinguished; rate-control and anticoagulation decisions cannot be safely made.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: New-onset atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent P waves with an irregularly irregular narrow-complex rhythm is diagnostic of AF — the definitive test, unavailable via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: New-onset atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A rapid ventricular rate defines the 'rapid ventricular response'; the measured rate is a clinician/device value, not reliably obtainable from this patient over chat.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: New-onset atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Irregularly irregular pulse + apical-radial deficit + variable S1 + absent P waves on ECG together are effectively diagnostic of AF — but every element requires clinician examination or an ECG and is therefore sealed from the telehealth encounter.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms AF versus flutter/SVT and screens for ischaemia; the single most important first investigation and the reason in-person assessment is required.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum electrolytes, renal function, full blood count
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Baseline before rate-control/anticoagulation; renal function guides DOAC dosing.
Excludes thyrotoxicosis as a reversible precipitant of new AF.
Screens for myocardial ischaemia as a precipitant/mimic given dyspnoea and presyncope in an older hypertensive adult.
Assesses structural heart disease and left atrial size to guide longer-term rhythm/rate strategy; not required emergently.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home fingertip pulse counting as a substitute for ECG
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A self-palpated pulse cannot confirm the rhythm or exclude ischaemia; relying on it instead of arranging an ECG would be unsafe.
